Okay, so welcome to module three. This is going to cover the entire first part of your enrollment call, which I call unpacking situational gestalts. In this lesson, we're going to uncover the source code, meaning what is it exactly that we are coaching in this part of the call? What are the milestones we are trying to achieve? How will we know we've actually cleared and are ready to move on to part two? Which is actualizing all the potential that has been built up. So we are here in part one unpacking the situational gestalt. The three milestones you could see at this bottom timeline are resonance, relevance, and resourcefulness. So we're going to break all three of those down way deeper in this session. And you'll really get a grasp of exactly what we are trying to coach our pre-clients towards. So once again, that is your goal of part one is to coach them into that experience of resonance, of relevance between what they want, their personal situation and your offer, how all three of those things weave together perfectly and resourcefulness, right? Which comes as a combination of both relief and this newfound sense of readiness. So let's begin. The main tool we're going to be using to accomplish these three milestones is as we said, unpacking the situational gestalt, which is going to be the main focus of the remaining lessons of this module. But for now, the source code lesson right here will help unpack what we mean by resonance, relevance, and resourcefulness, and essentially give you a chance to layer in whatever strategies and techniques you're already using or what you believe you can use to integrate in service of these milestones. So it's more about coaching these milestones, more about achieving that relevant to the resonance and that resourcefulness than it is the precise and specific techniques that you use to unlock them. So when we talk about resonance, what do we mean? Well, we mean three types of it. Resonance with you, your company, your values, resonance with your assessment of what is exactly going on in their life. Why aren't they getting exactly what they are intending to get, whatever that looks like. So your diagnosis and resonance with your prognosis and your prescription, how you feel things can be for them based on what you've heard, based on your intake and what you prescribe as being the best, most effective path forward based on everything that's already been put on the table. So those are the three main things you will be seeking to get resonance with on your enrollment calls and resonance with you specifically as a human that they're talking to will come through feeling a sense of trust, right? So the best that you're looking out for them and have their best interest in play, right? And this is all about that safety that we're establishing throughout. It's also going to come from liking who they are and how they feel in your presence, essentially experiencing themselves as a fuller, more confident version of who they are within your presence, right? We like hanging out with people who make us feel more alive, more powerful, more confident, more expressive of who we actually are. So this once again is what happens when you help them channel into their sense of power. Also, their dreams, goals, desires and hopes being made more likely, right? If they feel like they are more connected to a sense of possibility that their visions can be actualized through you, right? And through what you are encouraging them around through your prognosis of how things can be and your confidence that things could actually unfold brilliantly, whereas before they may not have felt that degree of confidence, that is what is going to create a massive sense of resonance. So the short way of saying this is we feel resonance around those who make us feel most alive and powered and in touch with an expanded sense of possibility that we wouldn't even dare strive to go for before meeting that person or before being in that person's presence. So trust and resonance are qualities ascribed to those we've experienced in sight and expansion around. Next, resonance with your assessment or diagnosis. This is going to come from fully elucidating why they've struggled in the past, having coherence, having some degree of meaning and reason behind why they haven't succeeded in getting what they want, why they've struggled despite all the effort and energy they've put into it, right? And this is also going to come from you demonstrating a level of empathy and a full understanding by just almost psychically knowing other elements of the problem that they perhaps haven't even volunteered, right? But it's just because you are so attuned to them and their situation that you can almost fill in the blanks. And if you could do that, that's going to create even deeper resonance like, oh gosh, how did they even know that about me? I didn't even volunteer that. So we're going to talk about that more in just a second. And then finally, getting their full body to agreement around the cause, right? If they can finally get this full agreement of, wow, this is why my business hasn't been taken off. Wow, this is why my relationship has been stuck in neutral. Wow, this is why I'm not in the shape I want to be in, right? And that lands fully and resonantly. This is what lands as insight and creates once again, such a new expanded experience of what can now be possible on the other side of it. Why? Because confusion is the land of frustration, helplessness, hopelessness and despair, whereas gaining clarity is an empowering event. It takes us from this land of unmitigated symptoms to a clear cause and condition that we can finally rally around. So this is what invites that sense of relief, resourcefulness and courage to pursue it. Likewise, feeling resonance around the path forward, the prescription reinforces the feelings of resourcefulness and courage. So let me explain that more. We're able to get more resourceful. We're able to channel more energy, more time, more money, more everything, essentially more time in our lives when we have clarity exactly around how those resources will be utilized. So a simpler way of putting this is that no one wants to waste time, energy or money until they have a high degree of confidence that it will actually pay off. So meaning until they actually feel resonance around your prescription, you can't expect them to channel their resources of time, energy and money in order to pursue that path. So your prescription essentially lines up with what your program or service is going to be. But in this stage of the call in part one, obviously we're not delivering the offer, we're delivering the prescription. This is what you need, right? And it's most simplest form. And this is what creates resonance that unlocks all these things we just talked about. So how does this work? Let's actually look at it in action. So let's say I am talking to somebody who is struggling to build their list and I listen to a story of their struggle, right? And then I respond. And what I'm hearing is that you really want to grow your audience, but you've been struggling to stay consistent with your podcast, guest posting and add strategies and kind of beating yourself up for it, feeling like you don't have the discipline to stay consistent. And they might say, yep, that's exactly, that's exactly it, right? So that's where we're gaining resonance around the symptoms. And then I might follow up and I bet along with that, you've also had the experience of starting a bunch of list building courses, working diligently in them until module three and you say, this just isn't clicking, right? And then that feeling, that sting of having just another course that is sitting unused as a sunk cost. And they might say, yep, nailed it. And that's where I'm getting resonance around these other elements that they didn't even volunteer, right? But that I'm aware of because I'm so attuned to what that situation typically looks like. And then I might follow up. Yes, so that's all pretty normal. And I'd actually want to propose that you've actually been right to slap on the brakes to stop the bleeding of lost time and money. So many people just continue pushing through that program and persevering when in reality it only puts them in a deeper hole, right? So I actually give them relief, show them that maybe they've actually made a wise decision and they might respond with an, oh, wow, never thought of it that way. So this is where I'm re-empowering them, where they perhaps previously felt some degree of shame or failure. And I'd say, well, tell me if I'm correct, but your real problem isn't staying consistent with your audience growth strategies. It's actually your fear that your systems and sales funnels aren't developed enough to make the most out of every lead that comes through. And they might say, oh my God, that's exactly it, right? And this is where I'm getting resonance around a diagnosis that they just didn't have before. Then I might say, my thought is that if you invested a month creating airtight systems and a high performing conversion funnel that you knew every lead would enter, you'd feel more confident than ever scaling your audience growth, knowing you'd at least have the opportunity to convert everyone who came in and be able to track and optimize ROI, right? And they'll say, yeah, totally. And that will be resonance around the prescription and a prescription that would be linked to my offer later. So that's how it all works. That is resonance. Now we're going to dive into relevance. So the main types of relevance we're going to be looking for is relevance between what they want, their entire personal situation and background on this situation. And of course, your offer and it's going to be all about linking all these things together. So relevance as a whole comes from feeling like they put their entire situation on the table, including past problems and personal doubts and having confidence that your prescription, meaning your offer actually has taken all these things into account that they're all relevant that they're all tied together. So this is super important and something that gets missed quite often. But even if after the first 10 minutes of talking to someone, you feel certain, absolutely certain you can help them. And do them the disservice of saying so until they fully emptied themselves of the problem. Catharsis, having the feeling and sense that you put it all on the table, every doubt, every problem, every regret, every piece of remorse, every piece of frustration, every piece of sadness, every lost opportunity, feeling catharsis around putting all that on the table is often the first step towards total resolution. You wouldn't trust a doctor's prescription if he cut you off before you put the full stack of symptoms on the table. Why because it would leave room for doubt and uncertainty around the accuracy of the diagnosis and the prescription. So to make your offer feel most relevant, make sure they put absolutely everything on the table when unpacking the Gestalt, right? Ask is there anything else you'd like to add about this? Do you feel complete? What else have you tried around this? How do you feel that wasn't effective? The more they can unpack, the more relevance there's going to be between where they're at and the solution you're going to propose later on the call. So when an expert has taken a full, a complete inventory of the problem in all its facets, emotional, mental, physical and the full dimensionality of it and has given a favorable prognosis with the sense of, yeah, I've seen this before and I'm confident we can fix this. We begin to feel a wave of relief, a wave of relief, sorry, as if this massive chasm has opened and we're now able to play a new, better game where we can't lose. So resourcefulness therefore becomes a combination of one, the relief they'll feel once everything is on the table, right? And they feel relevant and they have a clear resonant diagnosis, prognosis and prescription which leads to them having permission to reconnect to their desire and the possibility of its actual attainment, which is to say trust that it's safe to invest resources to achieve that outcome. Then finally, their readiness to rechannel their resources of time, money, attention, energy into a more favorable direction. So resourcefulness comes as an emergent of all these qualities and it is a prerequisite for positive action, meaning an enrollment. So the more of it that can be cultivated in the first half of the call, the more resourceful they can feel, the greater their capacity will become to take action on your offer. So wherever possible, boost their sense of resourcefulness by asking questions like this, that stuckness you're talking about wasn't there, what would you be doing, right? Get their minds out of this idea of being stuck. If two, if you knew there was an effective path, you know, or assuming there's an effective path, how would you find the energy to pursue it or acknowledging there is an effective path? How would you find the energy to pursue it? And finally, if you knew you couldn't fail, would you be able to re gather the energy to pursue this? This is a list of questions, use variations on this, but it's a great way to re invite resourcefulness, especially when you sense that they might be a little bit doubtful around the possibility of a path forward. So that is essentially it for the source code. These are the things we're going to be coaching, resonance, relevance and resourcefulness. You can almost forget about everything else, right? But in part one, if you have other tools on the table to coach through those milestones by all means do it the way I've been leading it, the way I've been teaching it is in a method called unpacking the situational gestalt and over the course of this module, we're going to go deeper into every aspect of this and hopefully give you a new powerful tool that you'll feel confident integrating into your sales calls. So that's it. Take a breather and I will catch you in the next lesson. Bye for now.
